title: Today's AI Brief: OpenAI and Anthropic May Be Rivals, but Investors Aren’t Picking Sides
summary: A look at the rivalry between OpenAI and Anthropic, and the hunt for the brain's 'core algorithm'
imageAlt: Image from source article for OpenAI and Anthropic May Be Rivals, but Investors Aren’t Picking Sides
imageCaption: OpenAI and Anthropic May Be Rivals, but Investors Aren’t Picking Sides
imageSourceTitle: OpenAI and Anthropic May Be Rivals, but Investors Aren’t Picking Sides

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI brief. The world of artificial intelligence is moving fast, with new developments emerging every day. I'm looking at two, actually three, interesting stories that caught my attention recently. It's a lot to unpack, but stick with me, it's going to be a fun ride.

So, OpenAI and Anthropic have been battling for workers, customers, and public attention. They've been on opposite sides of policy proposals, and their CEOs were the only ones not to link hands among a dozen industry leaders. Despite their rivalry, their investors aren't picking sides. Both companies have secured significant funding, and it seems their investors are happy to support both players in the AI space. This raises interesting questions about the model economics and how these companies plan to differentiate themselves.
The Hunt for the Brain's 'Core Algorithm'

In another news item, Jeff Bezos is funding a project to find the brain's 'core algorithm'. This sounds like a wild hunt, but apparently, it's a serious effort to understand how our brains work. The idea is to identify a fundamental algorithm that underlies human cognition. I'm curious to see where this goes – it's not every day you hear about a tech billionaire backing a project to crack the code of human thought.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
